description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story agricultural building with a wide, gently sloped hip roof clad in reddish-brown tiles. The structure is primarily constructed from light-colored wooden planks, with visible support beams and posts at the open front. The front facade features a large, open entryway supported by two central wooden posts, allowing easy access for livestock or equipment. The side and rear walls are fully enclosed, with no visible windows or additional doors, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The roof overhangs the walls, providing extra protection from the elements, and the overall appearance is robust and functional, suitable for rural or peri-urban settings.
description: A rectangular wooden cowshed with a wide, reddish hip roof and an open front supported by wooden posts. The sides and rear are fully enclosed, emphasizing a practical, rural design.
description_view_0: The open front of the cowshed is visible, with two central wooden posts supporting the roof and partial side walls enclosing the structure.
description_view_1: The fully enclosed gable end of the cowshed is shown, with no visible openings or features except the wooden plank wall and the sloped roof.
description_view_2: The long, fully enclosed side of the cowshed is visible, showing the uninterrupted wooden plank wall beneath the overhanging hip roof.
description_view_3: The opposite gable end is shown, also fully enclosed with wooden planks and the sloped roof, mirroring the other end.
attribute_door_style: open front entryway with no visible doors, supported by wooden posts
